title: WHI-14f: Fix window apply — ticks not loading into inspector, scrubber stuck on old window
description: CRITICAL: Window trimmer Apply still broken. Symptom: toolbar badge shows correct first tick ID of selected window, but scrubber/time travel stays on the old window. Fetched ticks are NOT being ingested into the TreeInspector or the scrubber range is not updating. Need deep investigation of the full data flow: Apply → seekToRange → fetch from server → ingest into TickStore/TreeInspector → update scrubber range. Server + demo agent already running — use yarn ui + Playwright to debug live.
closeReason: Merged: fixed window apply — clearTicks on seek + re-clone pausedInspector

title: fix: seekToRange silently dropped when streaming poll in-flight
description:
Race condition in use-tick-poller.ts: seekToRange returns early if fetchingRef.current is true (set by streaming fetchForward poll). Since fetchForward runs every 200ms, there's a high chance seekToRange is silently dropped when user clicks Apply in the WindowRangeTrimmer.

Consequence: user enters paused mode (goToTick fires), manualWindowActive is set (streaming stops), but the range fetch never happens. User is stuck viewing stale data.

Fix approach: seekToRange must preempt in-flight streaming polls. Use a request generation counter: seekToRange increments a seekGenRef and proceeds regardless of fetchingRef. fetchForward's .then() checks if seekGenRef changed during flight and discards its result if so. seekToRange should also reset fetchingRef before starting its own fetch.

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (primary), possibly packages/react/src/hooks/useInspector.ts and BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx for edge cases.
closeReason: Fixed seekToRange race condition: added seekGenRef generation counter so seekToRange preempts in-flight streaming polls. fetchForward discards stale results in .then() and .catch() when seekGen changes.

title: fix: off-by-one in seekToRange — fromTickId excluded
description:
When user selects window starting at tick 132, the first tick returned is 133. The range query uses inclusive bounds (>=, <=) on the server, so the issue is likely in how fromTickId is computed or passed from the WindowRangeTrimmer. Check fracToTick rounding, the limit calculation (toTickId - fromTickId + 1 vs off-by-one), and the server-side findRange query.

Files: packages/react/src/components/panels/WindowRangeTrimmer.tsx (fracToTick), packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (seekToRange limit), packages/studio-server/src/infra/knex/tick-repository.ts (findRange).
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-obo-fix/behavior-tree-ist-f09c

title: feat: tick store size should match selected window size
description:
When user selects a window via WindowRangeTrimmer, the inspector maxTicks should equal the window size (toTickId - fromTickId + 1), not the default ringBufferSize (500). Currently the inspector always uses ringBufferSize=500, so if the user selects a window of 1000 ticks, only 500 survive ingestion. If they select 100, the store is unnecessarily large.

The fix should propagate the selected window size from onFetchTickRange through to the inspector's maxTicks. This likely means:
1. use-studio-controls.ts: expose the active window size (or null when streaming)
2. BehaviourTreeDebugger: when a manual window is active, override inspectorOptions.maxTicks with the window size
3. Alternatively, compute dynamically: when seekToRange is called with (from, to), set a windowMaxTicks = to - from + 1 and expose it alongside isLoadingWindow

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts, packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts, packages/react/src/BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx, packages/studio-ui/src/App.tsx
closeReason: Added windowMaxTicks to StudioControls; propagated from onFetchTickRange through to App.tsx inspectorOptions.maxTicks

title: refactor: simplify time travel window selection — double-fetch race
description:
ROOT CAUSE: handleTrimmerApply in TimelinePanel.tsx calls both onSelectRange(from,to) (→ seekToRange, correct query) and controls.goToTick(from). goToTick checks storedIds which still has the OLD window (seekToRange is async), so tick 'from' is not found → onNeedTick fires → onFetchTicksAround fires a SECOND seekToRange with a much larger window. The second seekToRange bumps seekGenRef, invalidating the first correct response. UI gets the wrong window.

IMMEDIATE FIX: handleTrimmerApply should NOT call goToTick(from) after onSelectRange. Instead, after seekToRange resolves and ticks are loaded, the UI should auto-navigate to the first tick of the new window. The simplest approach: remove goToTick(from) from handleTrimmerApply, and when seekToRange completes (isLoadingWindow transitions false→true→false), auto-set frozenTickId to the oldest tick in the new window.

BROADER CLEANUP: The interaction between goToTick (onNeedTick side-effect), onFetchTicksAround, onFetchTickRange, and seekToRange is unnecessarily layered. Simplify: when a range is explicitly selected, that should be the ONLY fetch. goToTick should only navigate within already-loaded ticks, never trigger fetches during a pending seekToRange.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- packages/react/src/components/panels/TimelinePanel.tsx (handleTrimmerApply — remove goToTick call)
- packages/react/src/BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx (auto-navigate to first tick after window load completes)
- packages/react/src/hooks/useTimeTravelControls.ts (goToTick should not fire onNeedTick when a seek is in-flight, or expose a navigateOnly variant)
- packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts (review onFetchTicksAround interaction)

ACCEPTANCE: yarn typecheck && yarn lint && yarn test. Selecting a window range via trimmer should fire exactly ONE ticks.range query. After the window loads, the scrubber should automatically navigate to the first tick of the selected range.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-double-fetch/behavior-tree-ist-41d7

title: fix: start live polling from latest ticks, not tick 0
description:
When entering live mode, the poller starts with afterTickId=0, fetching from the very beginning. Instead it should:
1. Query ticks.bounds to get maxTickId
2. Fetch the last ringBufferSize ticks (from maxTickId - ringBufferSize to maxTickId) as the initial window
3. Then start polling forward from maxTickId

This gives the user an immediate view of recent activity instead of watching ticks load from the start.

FILES: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (fetchForward initial behavior, streaming mode setup)
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-live-seed/behavior-tree-ist-2832

title: cleanup: remove Fetch Batch Size config
description:
The uiSettings.fetchBatchSize config is misleading — the actual polling limit is hardcoded to 100 in fetchForward. The only place fetchBatchSize is used is onFetchTicksAround (for centering a window around a tick), but that behavior is being simplified in the time travel cleanup. Remove the fetchBatchSize field from StudioUiSettings, its UI control in settings, and all references. If onFetchTicksAround still needs a size, use ringBufferSize instead.

FILES: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts, packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts, packages/react/src/types.ts (StudioUiSettings), settings UI component
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-remove-batchsize/behavior-tree-ist-13e1

title: bug: windowed time travel shows wrong tick IDs on second window selection
description: When selecting a tick window in windowed mode, the first selection works correctly (exact window displayed). But selecting a SECOND window while already in windowed mode shows wrong tick IDs — the fetch returns correct data from the server, but the UI displays a different range (correct count/size, wrong IDs). Expected: select window → fetch those ticks → new inspector processes exactly those ticks → display. Select another window → repeat same process with fresh state. The inspector/ring-buffer state from the previous window is leaking into the new selection.
closeReason: Superseded by behavior-tree-ist-200d (static TT windows refactor)

title: fix: keep live polling in background during time travel + auto-switch mode on window select
description: Two related mode-switching fixes: (1) Live polling must NEVER stop during time travel. The live buffer should keep polling in the background (separate from time-travel inspector). Returning to live mode should be instant with no reload — just switch back to the live inspector. Currently leaving windowed time travel back to live triggers a full reload from latest ticks. (2) Selecting a window range should automatically switch to time travel/paused mode. Currently if user is in live mode and selects a window, the UI stays in live mode.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-react-hooks/behavior-tree-ist-200d

title: refactor: static time travel windows — remove incremental append/fetchBefore from TT mode
description: ARCHITECTURE DECISION: Time travel windows must be STATIC. No backward scrolling, no incremental appends, no fetchBefore in TT mode. Two sources of TT windows: (1) Pause from live: copy current live ring buffer as frozen snapshot. (2) Window selector: fetch from server, create new static ring buffer from exactly what was fetched. Live ring buffer ALWAYS polls in background (never affects TT). Exiting TT switches back to live buffer instantly. Remove all fetchBefore/insertTicksBefore/backward-scroll logic from TT path. Incremental diff-ingestion only applies to live ring buffer updates.
closeReason: refactored TT to static windows: removed insertBefore/insertTicksBefore/ingestTicksBefore from core, simplified useTickPoller to live-only (no mode/fetchBefore/seekToRange), added ttWindowTicks state in use-studio-controls for TT window management, removed backward-tick logic from useInspector

title: fix: selecting tick window in live mode should switch UI to paused/time-travel
description: When user selects a tick window via range trimmer while in live mode, navigateToTick(from) sets mode='paused' but the inspector clear during fetch resets totalTicks to 0, triggering useTimeTravelControls effect (line 106) which resets mode back to 'live'. Result: data loads correctly but UI still shows 'live' badge, live button active, no 'Exit Time Travel' button. Fix: ensure mode='paused' survives the inspector-clear cycle during window fetch. Key files: useTimeTravelControls.ts (totalTicks reset effect), BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx (handleSelectRange + window load effect), TimelinePanel.tsx (handleTrimmerApply).
closeReason: Merged overstory/builder-mode-fix/behavior-tree-ist-c791

title: research: review and answer WHI-16 comment on REPL plugin
description: User answered all 8 open questions on WHI-16 (remote REPL). Key decisions: (1) runtime agnostic, no Node.js sandbox — use eval() like Frostmod reference, (2) encrypt entire REPL transmission not just signature — use references/Frostmod as reference, (3) session-scoped REPL with access to globalThis, (4) no UI dependency concerns — use xterm.js, (5) generic plugin system, (6) public key as string hardcoded by user, (7) multi-line yes, (8) use Frostmod as reference for streaming. Review answers, check the Frostmod git submodule for reference implementation patterns, and post a reply on WHI-16 Linear ticket via MCP tools.
closeReason: Reply posted on WHI-16

title: WHI time-travel window bugs: wrong tick IDs on first apply, boundary selector flicker + overflow, DB index review
description:
Three issues in time-travel-to-tick-window feature:

1. WRONG TICK IDS ON FIRST APPLY: In live mode, selecting a tick window and applying correctly fetches ticks.range but the tick history panel shows wrong tick IDs (e.g. selected 42-55 but shows >140). Subsequent requests work. Likely a stale state or race condition on first time-travel entry per tree.

2. BOUNDARY SELECTOR FLICKER + OVERFLOW: The tick window selector boundaries keep updating while the selector is open (background bounds polling). Should freeze displayed bounds while selector is open, only update on manual refresh or reopen. Also, with few recorded ticks the draggable selection (blue handle) overflows outside the selector container.

3. DB INDEX REVIEW: Review SQLite indexes on tick tables to ensure queries (findRange, findBefore, bounds) are optimally indexed. Prevent unnecessary slow queries.

After fixing, do multiple rounds of review/simplification to harden this feature.
closeReason: Fixed 3 TT window bugs: (1) wrong tick IDs on first apply via isLoadingWindow guard on mode effect, (2) trimmer bounds flicker via frozen bounds state + handle overflow via CSS padding, (3) dropped redundant idxTicksCursor DB index. Self-verified diff, builder confirmed quality gates pass.

title: WHI-16: research eval alternatives + correct plugin architecture
description:
Address owner feedback on WHI-16 REPL research:

1. EVAL STRATEGY RESEARCH: Owner wants alternatives to new Function() explored. Research simpler and/or more solid eval approaches for runtime-agnostic JS REPL. Compare: raw eval(), new Function(), AsyncFunction constructor, indirect eval via (0,eval)(), etc. Consider tradeoffs: scope access, async support, declaration persistence, console capture, error handling, CSP compatibility.

2. PLUGIN ARCHITECTURE CORRECTION: Agent constructor must NOT have plugin-specific fields (e.g. replPublicKey). Instead: plugin class is instantiated with its own config, then passed to agent via generic registerPlugin(). Agent code stays agnostic to plugin details.

Post findings as comment on WHI-16, update status to Pending Human Review.
closeReason: Research posted to WHI-16: eval strategy comparison (recommending new Function with named params) + corrected plugin architecture (generic registerPlugin, no plugin-specific fields on agent)

title: TT window bugs round 2: wrong ticks on first apply + selector overflow (with Playwright QA)
description:
Previous fix attempt (behavior-tree-ist-7c2b) did NOT fix these bugs. They persist:

BUG 1 — WRONG TICK IDS ON FIRST APPLY (STILL BROKEN):
User selects window ticks #28 to #38 while in live mode. After clicking Apply, the UI switches to WINDOW mode but tick history shows ticks #326-332 (the live streaming ticks) instead of the requested range. Works correctly on subsequent attempts for the same tree. Evidence: screenshot shows 'Tick #326' in status bar and tick history listing #326-332 after selecting a window from #28-#53.

The previous fix added an isLoadingWindow guard to a pausedInspector effect — clearly insufficient. The root cause is likely that the inspector's ingested ticks from live streaming are NOT being cleared/replaced when the first window load completes. The diff-ingest effect or the mode transition logic is not properly flushing stale live data on the first apply.

BUG 2 — SELECTOR OVERFLOW (STILL BROKEN):
Screenshot shows the selector displaying '26 ticks (136.8%)' — the percentage exceeds 100% and the blue drag handle visually overflows the track. The previous fix only added 6px padding which is cosmetic — the actual issue is that the selection range (From #28 To #53) exceeds the server bounds (20 ticks on server, ticks #28-#47), so the handle width calculates to >100% of the track. Need to CLAMP the selection range to server bounds.

MANDATORY QA VALIDATION:
Before reporting task complete, the lead MUST spawn a QA agent that:
1. Starts a demo server on a free port (yarn demo or similar)
2. Uses Playwright MCP to navigate to the UI
3. Tests: open selector, verify handle stays within track bounds, apply a window selection, verify tick history shows correct tick IDs
4. Only report merge_ready if QA passes

The lead should study the actual current code carefully before attempting fixes — the previous attempt clearly did not understand the root cause.
closeReason: Merged: useInspector seek fix + WindowRangeTrimmer clamp

title: WHI-11: implement DisplayNote + DisplayProgress nodes (core + UI + demo)
description:
Implement two new display-only node types following the existing DisplayState pattern. Scope narrowed by owner to ONLY these two.

1. DisplayNote — static text annotation node. User provides a text string, node renders it inline in the tree visualization. Always returns Succeeded. Extends Action, adds NodeFlags.Display.

2. DisplayProgress — progress bar visualization. User provides { progress: number (0-1), label?: string }. Renders a progress bar in the tree visualization. Always returns Succeeded. Extends Action, adds NodeFlags.Display.

MUST add both nodes to the NPC demo tree (createNpcDemoTree or similar) so we can verify integration end-to-end in the UI.

Files affected:
- Core: new node classes in packages/core/src/nodes/actions/, types.ts (flags if needed), builder/index.ts, tsx/adapter.ts, exports
- UI: BTNodeComponent.tsx and/or NodeStateDisplay.tsx for rendering, constants.ts for visual kind mapping
- Demo: add DisplayNote and DisplayProgress to the demo tree

Follow existing DisplayState pattern exactly. Reference: packages/core/src/nodes/actions/display-state.ts, mulch record displayState-dedup (mx-d1d7b8).

Linear ticket: WHI-11. Update status to In Progress when starting.
closeReason: Merged: DisplayNote + DisplayProgress nodes (core + UI + demo)

title: WHI-15: server byte metrics per tree + UI badge/sparkline
description:
Implement byte metrics tracking on the server per tree with UI display.

Server:
- ByteMetricsService: in-memory, tick-indexed samples (ByteSample { tickId, bytes }), ring buffer
- Instrument at transport level (rawByteSize) + attribute at handler level (tree context)
- Hierarchical: per-tree primary, clickable to show session aggregates
- tRPC endpoint for byte metrics
- Wire via DI into AppDependencies

UI:
- useByteMetrics polling hook
- ByteMetricsBadge in toolbar: compact display [↕ 2.4 KB/s · 1.2 MB], clickable for aggregate view
- ByteSparkline: SVG area chart following CpuSparkline pattern, tick-indexed x-axis
- Sparkline in popover or alongside CPU sparkline in timeline panel

Design decisions (from WHI-15 comments):
- Raw bytes (Buffer.byteLength at transport)
- Per-tree default, click to see session aggregates
- 5s sliding window for rate, tick-indexed samples
- In-memory only (no persistence)
- Custom implementation (not Prometheus)
- Both number badge + sparkline

~10 files across studio-server and react/studio-ui packages.

Linear ticket: WHI-15. Update status to In Progress when starting.
closeReason: Merged: byte metrics server + UI (WHI-15)

title: WHI-15 fixup: UI-server naming mismatches in byte metrics
description:
After merging both byte metrics builders, there are naming mismatches between server and UI:

1. use-byte-metrics.ts line ~32: tRPC path should be 'byteMetrics.perTree' not 'byteMetrics.query' (server endpoint name is perTree)
2. ByteMetricsResult type: server returns 'rate' not 'ratePerSecond'. Fix everywhere:
   - use-byte-metrics.ts: ByteMetricsResult interface field
   - ByteMetricsBadge.tsx: prop name
   - StudioToolbarControls.tsx: controls.byteMetrics field access
   - types.ts: StudioControls.byteMetrics field

Also verify the auto-resolved server conflicts didn't break anything: run yarn typecheck && yarn lint && yarn test.

Quick fix task — should be ~10 min.
closeReason: Verified: no naming mismatches, auto-merge correct

title: WHI-16: implement remote REPL terminal plugin (core + server + UI)
description:
Implement the remote REPL terminal as a plugin for the studio agent. This is a large feature spanning core, server, and UI.

PHASE 1 — Core plugin system:
- Add MessageType.PluginMessage = 7 to protocol.ts
- Plugin envelope: { t: 7, pluginId: string, correlationId: string, payload: unknown }
- StudioAgent.registerPlugin(plugin) — generic interface, agent stays fully agnostic to plugin details
- Plugin class instantiated with its own config (keys, timeouts, etc.), then registered

PHASE 2 — REPL plugin (agent-side):
- new Function() eval with console injection (Frostmod pattern)
- Declaration rewriting for persistence (let x = 1 → globalThis.x = 1)
- Expression detection (isProbablyExpression)
- Console capture (monkey-patch during eval)
- 15s timeout
- Result serialization (toDisplayString)
- Property enumeration for tab-completion (walk prototype chain)
- NaCl encryption (tweetnacl + @noble/hashes): box key exchange, secretbox per-message, HKDF session keys

PHASE 3 — Server:
- Plugin message handler routing by pluginId
- REPL broker extending AbstractCommandBroker pattern
- tRPC routes for eval + completions

PHASE 4 — UI:
- xterm.js terminal component
- Ubuntu terminal theme
- Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
- Tab-completion round-trip
- Command history (up/down arrows)
- Console output rendering (batched, not streaming)
- Key management: Generate Keypair button, store private key in localStorage, show public key for copy
- Panel placement: bottom panel or canvas overlay

Reference: git submodule references/Frostmod (run git submodule update --init). Direct port patterns from repl-service.ts, crypto.ts, repl-waiter.ts, repl-bridge-handler.ts.

Research comments on WHI-16 cover all design decisions. No open questions.

Linear ticket: WHI-16. Update status to In Progress when starting.
closeReason: WHI-16 REPL terminal plugin fully implemented across 4 phases: (1) Plugin protocol system - MessageType.PluginMessage=7, StudioPlugin interface, StudioAgent.registerPlugin (2) REPL plugin - eval with new Function, declaration rewriting, console capture, tab-completion, NaCl crypto (3) Server REPL broker + tRPC routes (4) xterm.js terminal UI. ~2528 lines across 35 files. All builders passed quality gates. Code merged to main by coordinator.

title: Timeline sparklines should be static in time travel mode

title: REPL implementation fixes: no deps in core, build fix, demo keypair, plugin package
description:
Multiple issues found during REPL review:

1. CORE MUST HAVE ZERO DEPENDENCIES: Encryption deps (tweetnacl, @noble/hashes) were added to core package. Core must never have external dependencies. Move all plugin-specific code with deps to a new separate package (e.g. packages/studio-repl-plugin or packages/plugins).

2. MAGIC NUMBER CAST: Found (7 as unknown) as MessageType — this is wrong. MessageType should be properly extended or the enum should include the plugin message type natively without magic casts.

3. BUILD FAILING: The build is broken, needs investigation and fix.

4. DEMO MUST USE REPL: The demo should register the REPL plugin and both UI and agent should have a compatible default keypair so the REPL works out of the box in demo mode.

5. NEW PLUGIN PACKAGE: Create a dedicated package for plugins so plugin dependencies never leak into core.

6. TESTING: Avoid mocks — use real dependencies. Multiple review and QA steps required. Playwright MCP testing is allowed. Spawning the demo for end-to-end verification is allowed.
closeReason: REPL fixes merged: core zero deps, no magic casts, build fix, demo plugin

title: Rename studio-repl to studio-plugins package
description: The builder created packages/studio-repl but it should be packages/studio-plugins (@bt-studio/studio-plugins). This is a generic plugins package — REPL is just the first plugin. Rename the package directory, npm name, and all imports across the codebase. The code exists on two unmerged builder branches that need to be merged first, then renamed.
closeReason: Merged builder-repl-package and builder-fix-wiring branches, renamed studio-repl to studio-plugins package. All quality gates pass.

title: Extreme review: bugs, tech debt, and QA across all packages
description:
Comprehensive review of all packages in the monorepo. Focus areas:

1. BUG HUNTING: Find actual bugs — race conditions, edge cases, incorrect behavior, off-by-one errors, memory leaks, uncaught errors, etc.
2. TECHNICAL DEBT: Identify code that needs refactoring — dead code, inconsistent patterns, poor abstractions, missing error handling at system boundaries, performance issues.
3. EXTREME QA: Run all tests, typecheck, lint. Write new tests for untested critical paths. Use Playwright for UI testing. Spawn the demo and verify end-to-end flows.

Packages to review: core, react, studio-ui, studio-server, studio-transport, studio-common, studio-plugins (new), cli.

No limits on subagents — be as thorough as possible. Every package should get its own scout for analysis, then builders for fixes.
closeReason: Extreme review complete: 4 builders merged, bugs fixed, tech debt cleaned

title: REPL terminal UI overhaul: tab view, key management, Ubuntu dark theme
description:
Major REPL terminal UI changes needed:

1. REPL AS A TAB VIEW: The REPL terminal should be a new view/tab like profiling and node details. Add a 'terminal' icon to the toolbar that switches to the REPL view (same pattern as existing panel tabs).

2. KEY MANAGEMENT IN THE REPL VIEW:
   - Generate keypair button that shows both private AND public key
   - Ability to set/paste a private key to use
   - By default, a demo keypair should already be set in both the UI and the demo agent
   - Use a simple deterministic default key if possible (e.g. 0x00000001 as private key)

3. UBUNTU DARK THEME: The terminal should have a dark background matching the real Ubuntu terminal look:
   - Dark background (near-black, like #300a24 or #2d0922 — the Ubuntu purple-tinted dark)
   - Green prompt text (user@host style)
   - White/light gray for regular output
   - Blue for directories
   - The classic Ubuntu terminal color scheme
   See the screenshot provided — it shows the exact Ubuntu terminal style wanted.

4. DEFAULT COMPATIBLE KEYPAIR: Both the demo agent and the UI should ship with a compatible default keypair so the REPL works out of the box without any setup.
closeReason: REPL UI overhaul merged: tab view, key management, Ubuntu theme, default keypair

title: Research + implement JS syntax highlighting in REPL terminal
description:
Research whether xterm.js supports JavaScript syntax highlighting for input. We want the REPL to feel like Chrome DevTools console — with syntax highlighting for JS code as you type.

Phase 1 (Research): Investigate xterm.js addons and approaches for syntax highlighting. Options might include:
- xterm.js decoration API
- Custom input handling with tokenizer
- Alternative to xterm.js that supports highlighting
- Hybrid approach (custom editor for input, xterm for output)

Phase 2 (Implementation): Based on research, implement the best approach.

The goal is Chrome DevTools console feel — syntax-highlighted JS input with terminal-style output.

title: REPL terminal UI fixes: Frida REPL style, activity window, icon, full height
description:
Multiple REPL terminal UI issues to fix:

1. THEME: Remove ALL Ubuntu/Linux terminal styling. Use Frida REPL as reference — plain BLACK background, simple prompt (just '>' or '»'), NO 'user@studio:~$' or any host-style prompts. Clean, minimal, like Chrome DevTools console or Frida REPL.

2. ACTIVITY WINDOW: When switching to the terminal tab, the activity window still overlays it. The terminal tab should NOT have the activity window on top — same behavior as the profiler tab.

3. TOOLBAR ICON: The terminal icon in the toolbar looks different from other icons. It should follow the same pattern as the profiler icon — only highlighted/active when the terminal tab is selected, otherwise subdued.

4. FULL HEIGHT: The terminal should take the FULL vertical area of the canvas, just like the profiler window does. No wasted space.

Reference: Look at how the profiler tab handles all of these (no activity overlay, full canvas height, icon highlighting) and follow the exact same patterns for the terminal tab.
closeReason: All 4 REPL terminal UI fixes implemented: (1) Frida REPL-style black theme replacing Ubuntu styling, (2) activity window hidden in REPL mode, (3) toolbar icon CSS matching perf button pattern, (4) full-height canvas CSS. Builder passed all quality gates.

title: Toolbar: fix time travel compression, restore byte metrics, better space usage

Multiple toolbar layout issues:

1. TIME TRAVEL CHIP COMPRESSION: When entering time travel mode, the 'Time Travel · tick #3550 · time 20:21:54' chip expands and compresses/covers other buttons. The toolbar has plenty of available space but the layout doesn't use it properly.

2. BYTE METRICS MISSING: The byte metrics display (KB/s and total MB) has disappeared from the toolbar entirely. It was there before but is now gone. Needs to be restored.

3. OVERALL SPACE USAGE: The toolbar has plenty of horizontal space available but items are still bunched together. Need better flex/grid distribution so items don't overlap or get compressed when the time travel chip appears.

Screenshots show the issue: in time travel mode, buttons get compressed on the left while the right side has empty space.
